raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint against Barclays Bank for submitting false and misleading information to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) in response to my previous credit card dispute and for violating my rights under federal law. 

In their response to my original complaint, Barclays claimed that they had fully investigated my dispute and found no evidence to support it. However, they explicitly admitted in that same response that they did not offer me any method to submit documentation relevant to my case. They use language such as not being allowed to upload documents electronically yet offered no alternative such as a physically mailing address or fax. This admission directly contradicts their claim of conducting a proper investigation.

A fair investigation is impossible if the consumer is denied the ability to provide evidence.

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), 15 U.S. Code 1666, which is part of the Truth in Lending Act, card issuers are legally required to : 1. Acknowledge a billing dispute within 30 days, and 2. Conduct a good faith investigation and resolve the dispute within two billing cycles ( not exceeding 90 days ).

Barclays failed on both fronts. By refusing to give me a way to submit documentation, they violated the FCBA and knowingly misrepresented their investigations completeness in their CFPB response. 

In addition to these violations, I am now attaching supporting documents that Barclays failed to consider. These documents clearly demonstrate that the transaction in question was not an in-store purchase, contrary to their claim. The content and details of the documentation match the exact transaction that appeared on my account further proving that my original dispute was valid and that Barclays so-called investigation was not only incomplete, but intentionally dismissive.
